title: Alignment review: validate guide↔standard mappings + written rationale
context: The curriculum crosswalk (data-012) maps guides to standards, but mapping accuracy must be independently validated by a science/curriculum reviewer who can confirm the guide genuinely addresses the cited standard and that the written rationale is accurate and sufficient. This is a human review task, not automated validation. Depends on data-012 (mappings must exist before they can be reviewed).
objective: Conduct a systematic alignment review of the curriculum crosswalk produced in data-012: for each guide-to-standard mapping, verify that the guide genuinely addresses the cited standard, that the standard code and link are correct, and that the written alignment rationale is accurate and non-trivial. Produce a written alignment review report documenting findings, any incorrect or weak mappings flagged, corrections made, and final sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Every guide-to-standard mapping in the data-012 crosswalk is reviewed by a qualified science/curriculum reviewer (distinct from the crosswalk author)
Review confirms that each guide genuinely addresses the cited standard and that the written alignment rationale accurately explains how
Any incorrect standard codes, broken links, or weak/generic rationales are flagged and corrected before sign-off
The alignment review report documents: reviewer name and qualifications, frameworks reviewed, mappings checked, issues found, corrections made, and final sign-off date
The report is committed to the repository as a version-controlled artifact (not just a PR comment)
No verbatim restricted standard text was introduced during the review process; all corrections maintain the code+link+rationale-only model
output: A pull request adding the alignment review report (validating guide-to-standard mappings from data-012) with a qualified reviewer's sign-off, to the oer-science-labs repository.
